column2: Cannabis should be decriminalised.
Prostitution should be illegal.
Abortion is morally wrong.
All drugs should be decriminalised.
Certain ethnicities, genders, or sexual orientations, should sometimes be given preferential treatment.
It is sometimes acceptable to perform unethical experiments for medical research.
Free access to the internet should be provided by the state as a public utility.
Genetic modification of human embryos is immoral.
Religious values should inform law.
Faith schools indoctrinate children and should be restricted or banned.
Without religious values, there can be no right and wrong.
Governments should not favour one particular religion.
Publicly owned research institutions, such as space programs, should receive more public funding.
Essential utilities, like electricity and water, should be publicly owned.
All taxes should be abolished.
Currency should be abolished.
The government should regulate the economy to prevent monopolies.
Corporations should be trusted without regulation.
The government should be trusted.
Those with a greater ability to pay should receive better healthcare and education.
The manufacturing industry should be publicly owned.
People are ultimately more divided by class than nationality.
If technological automation is inevitable, the resultant unemployment is a positive outcome.
Land should not be a commodity to be bought and sold.
The rich are too highly taxed.
The only social responsibility of a company should be to deliver a profit to its shareholders.
There should be no minimum wage, as employers know best what a worker's labour is worth.
Welfare programs that sometimes benefit those too lazy to work should be reduced or eliminated.
Economic inequality is a greater issue than social inequality.
A person's gender identity may be different to their biological sex.
Torture, even during warfare, is morally wrong.
I support the death penalty for serious criminals, even if some are falsely accused.
My national identity is important to me.
Borders should not restrict human movement.
International tax systems should be implemented to mitigate tax avoidance.
Intellectual property is a legal fiction created by the state and should be abolished.
A united world government is a goal mankind should strive for.
Scientific research should be freely available to the public, rather than restricted or sold for profit.
Foreign-born migrants should be required to learn our language.
My nation is greater than other nations.
Military action that defies international law is sometimes justified.
My nation should not be dependent on other nations for resources.
Corporate exploitation of cheap foreign labour ultimately benefits everyone.
Foreign-born migrants should not have the right to vote.
Criminals should not have the right to vote.
Voting should be mandatory.
A region in which secession has democratic support should be recognised as an independent nation.
People should not worry about government surveillance if they have nothing to hide.
It is acceptable for the state to censor websites which speak negatively of the government.
Violent revolution is sometimes justified against a popular and democratically elected government.
Nuclear energy production should be phased out due to its environmental and health risks.
Wealthy corporations should be prohibited from financial involvement in politics.
Certain social media platforms should be owned and regulated by the government.
International wealth redistribution programs should be implemented to mitigate global inequality.
We should prioritise the protection of our own planet before we explore others.
Society was better in the past than it is now.
My political philosophy is derived from my faith.
A carbon tax should be implemented to encourage the free market to minimise greenhouse emissions.
Basic needs for survival should be unconditionally provided for free.
All authority should be questioned.
The state should subsidise private companies that invest heavily in scientific or technological research.
New technologies should be regulated to protect jobs they would otherwise make obsolete.
Revolution is the only way to achieve the society I desire.
Radical ideas can be dangerous, so it is best to change society incrementally.
The political system should be trusted to reform itself.
Politicians generally serve my interests.
Violent revolution is justified if the ruling party maintains control by undermining democracy.
I do not care how my nation is perceived by other nations.
Military service should be mandatory.
Technology developed through human suffering should not be used.
My nation should topple foreign governments which do not follow my nation's principles.
Foreign whistleblowers who reveal crimes committed by their government should be granted asylum.
Freedom of speech should be protected, even if it is offensive or untrue.
The police need more power to protect us from extremists.
It is never acceptable for protesters to break the law.
Voting is pointless.
Atheism should be enforced by the state.
The government should never interfere with religious traditions.
The environment cannot accommodate the current human population without a reduction in consumption.
The consumption of animal products should be phased out due to environmental and ethical concerns.
My nation's political system is dysfunctional and must be reformed.
I support lowering the legal voting age.
Elections should be publicly funded.
Universal services or incomes are desirable ways to distribute the productivity of an automated workforce.
Government officials should be selected through a democratic process.
I would rather be ruled by a dictator I agree with than an elected leader I disagree with.
Justice should be primarily rehabilitative, rather than punitive.
Sacrifices to living standards should be made if they are necessary to preserve the environment.
Abortion should be encouraged during pregnancies in which genetic illnesses are identified.
No one chooses their country of birth, so it is foolish to be proud of it.
Diplomacy is a better foreign policy asset than a strong military.
Globalisation is inevitable. There is no way to reverse modern hyper-connectivity.
Stereotypical gender roles are justified because they exist due to biological differences.
To make compromises with your political opponents is to abandon your principles.
The industrial revolution and its consequences have been a disaster for the human race.
Religious traditions hinder technological and social progress.
Direct democracy is a superior electoral system to representative democracy.
Religious attire should be required by law.
The wealthy should be taxed all of their income in excess of an upper limit.
Illegal immigrants, who are not political refugees, should be deported without exception.
